auth.subtitle: Circadian Rhythm Calculator
auth.tagline: Adjust your sleep schedule gradually, not abruptly. Based on chronobiology.
auth.loginError: Could not sign in. Please try again.
auth.googleError: Error connecting to Google. Please try again.
auth.privacyNotice: By signing in, you accept our terms of use. Sync does not share your data with third parties.
auth.guestBannerText: Your data is stored locally only. Sign in with Google to sync across devices.

settings.startDateTooltip: Date from which you begin adjusting your circadian rhythm. Used to calculate which day of the process you are on.
settings.initialSleepTooltip: Your actual starting sleep time. Sync calculates the advance from this point toward the goal.
settings.initialWakeTooltip: Your actual starting wake time. Used together with the initial sleep time to build the plan.
settings.currentScheduleTooltip: These times update automatically based on elapsed days, the consolidation pattern, and whether you were able to advance each day.
settings.targetSleepTooltip: The final time you want to reach. This goal is fixed and does not move as you advance.
settings.targetWakeTooltip: Target wake time. On the last advance, Sync automatically reduces the minutes to land exactly at this time.
settings.finalAdjustNote: Automatic final adjustment: the last advance day may use less than {{amount}}m to land exactly.
settings.paceTooltip: How fast you want to adjust your schedule. 30min is gentler; 50min is more demanding.
settings.consolidationTooltip: How often you advance again, counting the advance day itself. With 2: advance, consolidation, advance.
settings.dayTooltip: Current day of your adjustment process. Calculated automatically from your start date.

status.prestartTooltip: The plan hasn't started yet; check the start date.
status.advanceAppliedTooltip: Today is an advance day and the adjustment will be applied.
status.advanceNotAppliedTooltip: Today was an advance day, but it's marked as not achieved.
status.consolidationTooltip: Today is a consolidation day: you maintain the current schedule to assimilate it.
status.planNotStartedDesc: The advance plan hasn't started yet; you can review and adjust parameters.
status.goalReachedDesc: Maintain this schedule in consolidation to stabilize the rhythm.
status.advanceNotDoneDesc: You marked this day as not achieved; the schedule holds until the next advance.
status.consolidationDesc: Your body needs to adapt to the recent change.
status.lastAdjustDesc: Last automatic adjustment to reach the goal without overshooting.
status.readyToAdvanceDesc: Your body is ready for the next adjustment.

calculator.copyFailed: Could not copy: the browser doesn't support clipboard or permissions are missing.
calculator.copyFailedPermissions: Could not copy to clipboard. Check browser permissions.

calendar.couldAdvance: I was able to advance this day
calendar.markWhenReached: Mark the result when this day arrives
calendar.advanceNotAppliedNote: This advance will not be applied to the calculation.

onboarding.welcomeSubtitle: Your body wants to sleep at 3 AM. The world wants you up at 7.
onboarding.welcomeText: Sync is a circadian rhythm calculator that helps you adjust your sleep schedule
onboarding.welcomeNote: Based on chronobiology protocols. Free and tracker-free.
onboarding.howItWorksSubtitle: It's not an abrupt change. It's a gradual adjustment.
onboarding.advanceDaysDesc: We adjust 30-50 minutes earlier. Your body feels the change but doesn't resist it.
onboarding.consolidationDaysDesc: We maintain the schedule so your body assimilates it before the next adjustment.
onboarding.configureSubtitle: Set what time you eventually want to sleep.
onboarding.targetBedtimeQuestion: What time would you like to sleep? For example: 11:00 PM
onboarding.paceOfChangeQuestion: How fast do you want to adjust? 30min is gentler, 50min is faster.
onboarding.readySubtitle: Here's what your first week would look like:
onboarding.weeksEstimate: In 2-3 weeks you could be sleeping 2-3 hours earlier.

about.title: About Sync
about.subtitle: Sleep science for those who live at night
about.problemTitle: Your biological clock isn't broken. It's just out of sync.
about.problemP1: If you're one of those people whose "day really starts after midnight," you're not alone. There are millions of people whose natural circadian rhythm is offset from the standard social schedule.
about.problemP2: Developers who code best at 2 AM. Gamers who find their flow when everyone else is asleep. People with delayed sleep phase who've been fighting their alarm for years.
about.problemConclusion: Science has a name for this: <1>late chronotype</1>. And it's not a flaw. It's a real biological variation.
about.scienceTitle: Adjusting your circadian rhythm doesn't require willpower. It requires strategy.
about.scienceP1: Your circadian rhythm is governed by the suprachiasmatic nucleus — a cluster of neurons in your brain that responds primarily to light. But also to other <1>zeitgebers</1> (time cues): temperature, food, and yes, melatonin.
about.scienceBoxTitle: Sync uses the phase advance technique:
about.scienceBoxText: Instead of trying to sleep 3 hours earlier overnight (which fails 90% of the time), we advance your schedule gradually — typically 30-50 minutes every two days.
about.scienceFootnote: Clinical studies show that gradual phase advance has success rates above 80% for adjusting sleep schedules.
about.audienceTitle: Designed for those the world wasn't designed for
about.gamersDesc: Your peak performance doesn't have to be at 3 AM. Gradually adjust to have energy when you need to be online.
about.devsDesc: That deep flow state you find at midnight can exist at 10 PM. You just need to recalibrate your internal clock.
about.dspsDesc: If your body "naturally" wants to sleep at 3 AM and wake at noon, Sync helps you shift that rhythm toward more functional hours.
about.shiftDesc: The transition between night and day shifts is brutal. A phase advance strategy can significantly reduce circadian disruption.
about.howTitle: Two types of days, one clear objective
about.howIntro: Sync alternates between two modes to recalibrate your biological clock without stress:
about.howAdvanceDesc: We adjust your target schedule 30-50 minutes earlier. Your body feels the change but doesn't resist it.
about.howConsolidationDesc: We maintain the same schedule so your body fully assimilates it before the next adjustment.
about.madeByTitle: Made by someone who's been there
about.madeByP1: Sync was born from a personal need. After years of fighting a biological clock that seemed "wrong," I discovered it wasn't broken — it just needed the right strategy.
about.madeByP2: It's a free tool because sleep health information should be accessible. No premium. No subscription. No "unlock features."
about.disclaimerText: Sync is a planning calculator based on established chronobiology protocols. It is not a medical device, it does not replace the advice of a health professional, and it is not designed for severe sleep disorders that require clinical intervention. If you experience chronic insomnia, sleep apnea, or symptoms of narcolepsy, consult a sleep medicine specialist.
about.ctaText: Improve your sleep. Don't fight your biology.

save.savedPartial: Partial save: could not validate all fields.
save.savedPartialError: The server responded, but the complete save could not be verified.

save.localFallbackError: Could not save to server. A temporary local copy was saved.
save.loadLocalFallback: Could not connect to the server. A temporary local copy was loaded.
save.loadFailed: Could not load saved data from the server.

validation.invalidConsolidationDays: Invalid consolidation days; 2 days will be used.
